palettes.name: Early Mythological Period
palettes.emotional_character: Archaic, mythic, surrealist-influenced. Biomorphic forms emerge from warm earthy grounds.
palettes.technique_context: Oil on canvas, figurative abstraction, Surrealist automatism influence.

palettes.name: Transitional Multiforms
palettes.emotional_character: Explosive transition—color finding its freedom from form.
palettes.technique_context: Moving toward pure color rectangles, forms dissolving.

palettes.name: Classic Orange Yellow Period
palettes.emotional_character: Radiant, expansive, solar. Rothko said he wanted viewers to weep before his color.
palettes.technique_context: Thin luminous oil glazes on raw canvas. Edges feathered to create breathing quality.

palettes.name: Seagram Murals Dark Period
palettes.emotional_character: Oppressive, monumental, tragic. Rothko intended these to ruin appetite—commissioned for Four Seasons restaurant but donated to Tate.
palettes.technique_context: Large-scale murals, dark grounds, forms suggest doorways and architecture—temples of doom.

palettes.name: Rothko Chapel
palettes.emotional_character: Void, silence, death, transcendence. The Chapel commissioned by de Menils is Rothko's ultimate statement—14 near-black canvases in an octagonal nondenominational chapel.
palettes.technique_context: Dry egg tempera and oil, ultramarine and black pigments. Rothko died before installation.

palettes.name: Harvard Murals
palettes.emotional_character: Intense, academic-critical, brooding. The crimson dominance creates sustained emotional pressure.
palettes.technique_context: Five-panel mural for Harvard Holyoke Center. Faded dramatically due to fugitive pigments (lithol red).

palettes.name: Late Gray and Black Works
palettes.emotional_character: Resigned, elegy, farewell. Painted after aortic aneurysm. Gray/black works created in the last two years of his life—stark renunciation of color.
palettes.technique_context: Acrylic on paper, simplified. Last works before suicide February 1970.
